summary: The optimal first-line diagnosis for a child suspected of having acute appendicitis is an abdominal ultrasound because it effectively detects the condition without exposing them to harmful radiation. This method also offers better accuracy than other imaging techniques or non-imaging tests like chest x-rays. \boxed{A}
